title: Trichantha calotricha
taxonomy: Trichantha calotricha is a species of flowering plant in the family Verbenaceae. It is native to certain regions in the Americas. The classification of Trichantha calotricha includes its genus, which is also referred to as Trichantha. Further classification places this species in the order Lamiales. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its evolutionary relationships.

intro: Trichantha calotricha, also known as "Sarsaparilla", is a plant species native to Central and South America, characterized by its long, thin vines and heart-shaped leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects that can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, wounds, and skin infections. Trichantha calotricha contains bioactive constituents such as saponins, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its therapeutic benefits.
